rationales.stimulation: In the response he contrasts exciting work with predictability and reaffirms that predictability is what he wants most days.
rationales.security: He grounds himself by checking that rent and the Bausparvertrag contribution are on track, then says he chose this job because it is steady and predictable.

rationales.stimulation: He dismisses meetups and networking because they could lead to choices he would rather avoid, preferring the known shape of his current week.
rationales.security: He emphasizes the unlimited contract, fixed routines, and the fact that he does not want to trade something that works for an unknown opportunity.

rationales.achievement: In the response he names reaching senior developer within two years and stresses that there is a clear path with concrete steps.
rationales.security: He frames the Bausparvertrag, eventual home ownership, and other trackable milestones as the future that feels right to him.

rationales.achievement: He highlights two concrete competence wins in the same entry: a strong volleyball performance and finally fixing the CSV export bug.
rationales.security: He leaves the Biergarten early so tomorrow stays intact, then feels reassured that his spending pattern is steady and exactly what he expected.

rationales.stimulation: He rejects the startup scene because he does not want the uncertainty and churn that others present as the exciting option.
rationales.security: He defends the permanent contract, pension plan, fixed hours, and low failure risk as deliberate reasons to stay where he is.

rationales.self_direction: He takes satisfaction in organizing the entire day on his own terms and explicitly notes that no one has any claim over how he runs it.
rationales.security: Meal prep, cleaning, laundry, and packing the volleyball kit are all described as getting everything in order, which is what makes the day feel right.

rationales.achievement: He values the strong review, the exceeds-expectations ratings, and the clear path toward senior developer as evidence of competent progress.
rationales.power: He explicitly rejects management and higher-status career scripts, preferring to stay an individual contributor rather than pursue authority.
rationales.security: He wants to stay in the same stack, keep weekends protected, and let the raise feed the Bausparvertrag instead of chasing a riskier next step.
He immediately runs the rent numbers, protects savings marked for the future, and defines success as finding a predictable flatmate arrangement quickly.
rationales.conformity: His flatmate criteria center on low-disruption norm-following behavior: quiet, employed, pays on time, and no weeknight parties.

rationales.stimulation: Even with the appeal of Kotlin, remote work, and startup challenge, he closes the tab and treats that uncertainty as something to avoid.
rationales.security: He keeps returning to the permanent contract, pension, trusted role, and familiar codebase as reasons not to destabilize a situation that already works.
